2605.14741.title: Addressing Terminal Constraints in Data-Driven Demand Response Scheduling
2605.14741.paper_summary: Electrified chemical processes are incentivized by exposure to time-varying electricity markets to operate flexibly, but participating in demand response schemes can require satisfying terminal constraints over long horizons. Specifically, terminal constraints may be required when computing optimal schedules in order to preserve dynamic stability. Model-based optimization methods are computationally costly, and data-driven scheduling via reinforcement learning (RL) faces severe credit-assignment challenges. We integrate Goal-Space Planning (GSP) with Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (DDPG), using learned temporally abstract models over discrete subgoals to propagate value across extended horizons. Using a simulated air separation benchmark, we demonstrate the proposed approach improves sample efficiency over standard DDPG while satisfying terminal storage constraints, mitigating myopic control behavior.
2605.14741.paper_summary_zh: 電氣化化學過程受到時間變化的電力市場的激勵，以靈活運作，但參與需求響應計劃可能需要在長期內滿足終端約束。具體而言，在計算最佳時間表時，可能需要終端約束以保持動態穩定性。基於模型的優化方法計算成本高昂，而通過強化學習（RL）的數據驅動排程面臨嚴重的信用分配挑戰。我們將目標空間規劃（GSP）與深度確定性策略梯度（DDPG）結合，利用學習到的時間抽象模型在離散子目標上推廣價值，跨越延長的時間範圍。使用模擬的空氣分離基準，我們展示了所提出的方法在滿足終端儲存約束的同時，提高了樣本效率，減輕了短視控制行為。
2605.14741.author: Maximilian Bloor et.al.
2605.14741.authors: Maximilian Bloor, Martha White, Ehecatl Antonio del Rio Chanona, Calvin Tsay
